translated: And when the man saw that he could not prevail against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh, and the hollow of Jacob's thigh was put out of joint as he wrestled with him.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ owure‑o a te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kuŋurole̱ŋ wuye ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ko̱kɔ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ? Amaa fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a taa fo̱ ko̱kɔ amo̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.
translated: Moses and his father-in-law were talking and his father-in-law said to him. And he said to him saying, 'Why are you the only one judging them? But why do you sit alone all day? But if you listen to me what I will tell you, I will help you so that it will be good for you.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ma kine mò̱ ka ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, amɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a kyɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ kamɛɛ fe̱raa. Ne̱ n gye̱ ane̱ŋ, ɔnyare̱‑ɔ ya waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ, ɔ waa bɔye̱. Mò̱ ke̱be̱e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑ɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a waa kakye̱e̱kpa‑ɔ.”
translated: Moses, the law that you gave us says that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man should marry the widow. Now then, there were seven brothers. The first one married and died without having children. The second one married the widow, and he also died. The same thing happened to the seven brothers—they all died without leaving any children.

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ mbraa?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ma mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛko̱, ɔko̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ ywii, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ te̱ŋ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ kanɔ,
translated: And the young man asked him saying, "Which ones?" And Jesus answered him saying, "The ones that say, 'You shall not kill, you shall not commit adultery, you shall not steal, you shall not bear false witness,

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kii nsii-amɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.”
translated: But they did not want to give the elders food, and they did not want to give food to the elders.

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kii se̱sɛ timaa lo̱we̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, nare̱ ya fe ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ de‑o, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ sa atiripo̱. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa kapo̱tɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra so̱so̱ sa fo̱. Na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "You should not think that I have come to destroy the law, but rather, I have come to fulfill it completely, and you will keep it all until heaven and earth pass away. You should know rather, that God will give great reward to those who do it. And you will enter into my kingdom."

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ siidii kudu na bo̱ yɔ ya suŋ sa mò̱ kake amo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ baa sure si, ne̱ baa yɔ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya suŋ.
translated: If you are a true believer, you should not let anything prevent you from praying. If they refuse, if they do not allow you to pray.

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, ‘Anyare̱ mɔ ne̱ baa ba fo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ a suŋ kusuŋ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ a kpo̱ne̱ suŋ kusuŋ ibiri mɔ pɛɛɛ na kyɔwe̱ a da ane̱. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa fo̱ a ka ane̱ aa bamo̱ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.’
translated: They said, 'God has given us this land as our inheritance and the animals that live in it are for our benefit. And we have the right to use all the animals that God has given us. But we must not kill them carelessly at all.'

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ atanne̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n de kpa fe̱yɛ m ba amo̱ bo̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Bɛɛɛ a bo̱ fo̱ gya fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?’ ”
translated: You are completely foolish. How can you say that whoever touches you will die when you have not died yourself? Or are you saying that you are an immortal?

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kra mò̱ kayaagyi amo̱, ne̱ baa gyiiri mò̱ le̱e̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa mɔɔ mò̱.
translated: Moses went and returned to his father-in-law, and told him all that had happened, and he blessed him.

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ke̱fo̱re̱ serisɛ ko̱ ne̱ apwɛɛpo̱ de bo̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ baa kine ke̱mo̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱‑ɔ, ya ko̱ŋ bo̱ kii ke̱mo̱ ne̱ kaa le̱ŋ lɔŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ ye̱ra‑ɔ. Wuribware̱ ya sa ne̱ aa ba ane̱ŋ, ne̱ aa kpe̱ŋ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱baŋse̱.’ ”
translated: Then Jesus said, "Truly I tell you. If you go and tell your brother about his sin against you, 'If he listens to you and repents and you forgive him, then you have gained your brother and he will be with you in peace and you will be together. But if he does not come and does not accept it, then take witnesses.'"

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa gywii lamaŋ ne̱ ba nu mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ na mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ.
translated: And Jesus went and preached to the people in the synagogues and cast out their demons.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ na Farisii awuye‑o mɔ, ŋ yɛ ɛɛɛ, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱ fɛɛ. Nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye e̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱. Mfaanɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ i gyiiri mo̱ne̱ ke̱se̱bɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.]
translated: Some of the Pharisees and teachers of the law who were there said to him, "Teacher, we want to see a sign from you." But he answered them saying, "An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah, and just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth."

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: Mo̱ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ na Farisii awuye mɔ, m be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye ne̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ. Ke̱mo̱ so̱so̱ ne̱ ba ba siimeetii bo̱ laa, ne̱ kaa waa daŋ. Amaa swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ kaase̱ kifuniŋ da ki i gyo.
translated: When the Pharisees and teachers of the law saw this, they said to him that he eats with tax collectors and sinners. When he heard this, he answered them. Healthy people do not need a doctor, but sick people do. However, go and learn what this means.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ m buŋ aprakɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a sa mo̱ atɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a buŋ. Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mo̱ na mo̱ e̱ lɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kya mo̱‑rɔ. Saŋ ne̱ baa tii mo̱ tiikpa‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ laa mo̱.’
translated: If you do not give him your friend, and you refuse to give him, and you do not give him. If you do not give him what he wants, and you keep it from him. If someone comes to you at midnight and you refuse to give it to him.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ba kaduŋfwii bo̱ kyaŋ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ waagya‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ a tɔwe̱ abuse̱ŋse̱ŋ. Ane̱ maa lɛɛ kpa a ɔko̱ tɔwe̱ ko̱tɔko̱ pwɛɛ a ane̱ dɛɛ ŋu fe̱yɛ o gyi ke̱pɔ. Mo̱ne̱ a nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱‑ɔ, ɔ yɛ mò̱ aa Wuribware̱ ta.
translated: They went to the chief's house and found him sitting outside in the morning. And he said, "God has given me three sons. text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Fo̱ i nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ‑o, tɔwe̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ da fo̱‑ɔ gywii ane̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi Yeesuu‑o (Maak

text: Saŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o de Yeesuu ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gyaŋŋaa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kireenee maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a ye̱re̱ mò̱ waa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ so̱rɔ Yeesuu a kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ.
translated: When they were taking Jesus away, they met a man coming from the countryside, and his name was Simon from Cyrene. And the soldiers forced him to carry Jesus' cross.

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Ne̱ baa ba iwu bo̱ lo̱ kuwurepa bo̱ buŋ mò̱. Ne̱ baa taa kayii nare̱se̱sɛ bo̱ waa mò̱ kigyisesare̱e̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba gyɔŋŋɔ mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ ba waa mò̱ ŋwaagyise̱ŋŋe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Aŋsuma, nana, Yudaa awuye owure.”

translated: And Joseph took the body of Jesus and wrapped it in clean linen cloth. And he laid it in his own new tomb.

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a sa se̱ŋsa gywii akye̱e̱‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱. Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa Yeesuu ne̱ baa da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.
translated: Then the angel spoke to the women who had come. He said, "You should not be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was nailed on the cross and died."

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱nꞌ nare̱ me̱naŋ me̱naŋ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ŋkpɛɛ o yii Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ba ŋu mò̱. Mo̱ ase̱ŋ a lo̱we̱.”
translated: Therefore, brothers, select from among you seven men of good reputation, full of the Holy Spirit and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this task, while we brothers will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word. This proposal pleased the whole group.

text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ kwe̱e̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa a akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ lee‑ro. Ɔɔ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ mbraa ɔsapo̱‑ɔ.
translated: And the reason why they do this and they call it a sacrifice is so that when the rains come, they will ask God to help them with their farming. They call this asking for help from God.

text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kare̱ fe̱yɛ ako̱ŋ a de Deefid mò̱ aa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, oo lwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa, ne̱ baa sa mò̱ bodobodoo akuri ne̱ baa bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ ɔɔ saŋ e̱ko̱ sa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, na a mo̱ŋ gye̱ mbraa kikye aaa? translated: When you were small and insignificant in your own eyes, did not the Lord anoint you king over Israel, and the Lord delivered you from the hand of Saul, and gave you your master's house and your master's wives into your keeping, and gave you the house of Israel and Judah.
